title: Sanding Performance Image Scoring
summary: Compute pixel-level paint-removal scores from reference and sanded panel images, then write a structured CSV with quantity, uniformity, and composite metrics.
taskPrompt: You are completing a sanding-performance image scoring task on a Windows VM.¥n¥nInput files are staged in:¥n`E:¥¥agenthle¥¥engineering¥¥sanding_performance_scoring_instance_1¥¥base¥¥input`¥n¥nRead `algorithm_spec.md` in that directory. It defines how to use the reference images `panel_painted.png` and `panel_unpainted.png` to score:¥n- `panel_sanded_01.png`¥n- `panel_sanded_02.png`¥n- `panel_sanded_03.png`¥n¥nUse RGB image values and follow the formulas in `algorithm_spec.md`.¥n¥nWrite exactly one output file:¥n`E:¥¥agenthle¥¥engineering¥¥sanding_performance_scoring_instance_1¥¥base¥¥output¥¥sanding_scores.csv`¥n¥nThe CSV must have this header:¥n`panel_id,quantity_pct,uniformity_pct,composite_pct`¥n¥nIt must contain exactly one row for each sanded panel:¥n- `panel_sanded_01`¥n- `panel_sanded_02`¥n- `panel_sanded_03`¥n¥nRound the three numeric metric columns to one decimal place. Do not modify the files under `input/`.¥n
agentMustDo: Read the visible algorithm specification and five panel images from the staged input directory.
Compute quantity, uniformity, and composite sanding scores for each of the three sanded panels.
Write `sanding_scores.csv` under the runtime output directory with the required schema and one row per sanded panel.

inputFiles.name: algorithm_spec.md
inputFiles.description: Visible task algorithm and output schema.

evaluation: The evaluator reads the candidate `output/sanding_scores.csv` and hidden `reference/sanding_scores.csv`. Missing or malformed output returns `0.0`. Otherwise it checks three numeric metrics for each required panel ID and returns the fraction of the 9 metric checks within absolute tolerance `1.0`. The positive fixture scores `1.0`; the negative fixture scores `0.0`.
